title: USFK proposes South Korea as Regional Sustainment Hub for Taiwan contingency operations
summary: US Forces Korea Commander Brunson proposed establishing South Korea as a Regional Sustainment Hub for maintenance, repair, and overhaul of warships, missile systems, and drones to support potential Taiwan contingency operations.
contents:
## USFK Proposes South Korean Sustainment Hub for Indo-Pacific Operations

### Proposal Details
US Forces Korea Commander Xavier Brunson proposed on April 22 establishing South Korea as a Regional Sustainment Hub (RSH), expanding existing aircraft maintenance arrangements to include warships, missile systems, and drone repair capabilities. The concept builds on the Department of Defense's 2024 Regional Sustainment Framework.

### Taiwan Contingency Link
The RSH proposal would clarify South Korea's operational role in a potential Taiwan contingency by enabling forward-deployed maintenance and overhaul capabilities. Currently, the US returns many damaged or worn assets to the continental United States for repair — a process the RSH model aims to streamline significantly.

### South Korean Response
The Lee Jae Myung administration has not formally responded to the proposal. Lee has shown willingness for expanded regional defense roles, advocating for indigenous nuclear-powered attack submarines to counter PRC submarine activities and proposing OPCON transfer from US to Korean command.

### Strategic Sensitivity
South Korea maintains strategic ambiguity regarding cross-strait issues due to the PRC's economic significance. China previously imposed economic pressure on Seoul following the 2017 THAAD deployment, creating a cautionary precedent for expanded US-ROK cooperation aimed at China deterrence.

**Source:** ISW-AEI analysis, Asia Today/UPI
